content_parts.text:
You are an expert at extracting calendar events from emails.

**Current Date:** 2026-02-21

**Email Metadata:**
- Subject: Feb 11, 2026 Digest: 1 new message from Walnut Creek School District today
- From: Walnut Creek School District via ParentSquare <donotreply+f44b5b2b-0fe1-53bf-b5c1-2dada4954bce@parentsquare.com>
- Date Sent: 2026-02-12T02:35:15Z

**Instructions:**
1. Analyze the email content and any attachments (including PDFs, images, calendars)
2. Extract ALL calendar events mentioned — meetings, appointments, parties, closures, themed days, deadlines, etc.
3. For each event, extract:
   - Title: clear, descriptive event name
   - Start date/time (parse relative dates like "tomorrow", "next Friday")
   - End date/time (if mentioned)
   - Location: physical address, room, venue, or virtual meeting link. If no specific location is mentioned in the email, set to null. Do NOT infer or guess locations from the organization name or event type.
   - Full description with all relevant details
   - Whether it's an all-day event (true/false)
   - Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on clarity of information
   - Importance: "action_required" or "fyi" (see classification below)
   - Recurrence rule: for recurring events (weekly meetings, monthly reviews, etc.), provide an RFC 5545 RRULE string (e.g., "RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO"). Null for one-time events.

**Importance Classification:**
- **action_required**: Events that require the recipient to take action or change their schedule.
  Examples: closures (school closed, office closed), early dismissals, half days, deadlines,
  appointments, meetings, conferences, registration deadlines, performances to attend.
- **fyi**: Informational events that are nice to know but don't require action.
  Examples: themed dress-up days (pajama day, hat day), staff birthdays, spirit weeks,
  movie days, classroom activities the child participates in without parent involvement.

**Important:**
- If NO calendar events are found, set events_found=false and return empty events list
- Parse dates carefully using the current date as context
- Include uncertainty in confidence scores (e.g., 0.7 if time is ambiguous)
- Extract ALL events including themed days and informational items — classify them as "fyi"
- For PDFs: extract events from calendar grids, flyers, and schedules

**Examples of events:**
✓ Birthday party invitations (action_required)
✓ Doctor/dentist appointments (action_required)
✓ Meeting requests (action_required)
✓ School closures (action_required)
✓ Themed dress-up days at school (fyi)
✓ Staff birthdays (fyi)
✓ Conference registrations (action_required)

**NOT events (do NOT extract these):**
✗ Commercial transactions: receipts, shipping notifications, payment reminders, subscription renewals, promotional offers
✗ Administrative notices: terms of service updates, privacy policies, password resets, account statements, survey requests
✗ Content without actionable dates: newsletters, social media notifications, general announcements without specific event details
✗ Emails where event details (date, time) must be inferred or guessed from context — only extract events with explicitly stated dates and times

**Extraction rules:**
- Multi-day events (e.g., 3-day conference) should be ONE event with start/end spanning the full duration, not separate per-day events
- Do NOT create separate events for RSVP deadlines, early bird deadlines, or registration cutoffs — note them in the main event's description instead
- Only extract events with explicitly stated dates — do NOT infer dates from holidays, context, or subject lines

**Email Body:**
Daily digest created for Toni Melisma

1. WH Weekly eNews Feb 11, 2026: Enjoy the Long Weekend!

Posted by Amy Romem on Wednesday, Feb 11 at 5:15 PM in Walnut Heights School

WH Weekly eNews

February 11, 2026

Principal's Message

Good Evening WH Families!

Thank you to all of the families that supported WCEF's Turn Up the Heat. Our WCSD schools collectively raised almost $110,00. I will share final numbers for our school as soon as I have the update. We appreciate all of your support to our school programs and specials.

Reminder that February 12-16th is President's Weekend. There is no school tomorrow, Friday or next Monday. Enjoy your five day weekend!

Our teachers and staff will be spending time together tomorrow to learn about updates to our math curriculum & focus on student writing!

There were quite a few celebrations in classes today. Please check your child's backpack for uneaten treats and goodies.

Parent Survey

We are currently at 5% participation! Our goal is 75%! Please take a (few) minute to give us feedback as a school and district and complete the Youth Truth Survey. YouthTruth, a nonprofit organization that supports hundreds of schools and districts across the country, conducts an anonymous survey on our behalf.

Please take the survey before it closes at midnight on February 27 by clicking this link: ytsurvey.org/wcsdfamily26.

It's a great day to be a Cougar!

Sincerely,
Amy Romem, Principal

WH Special Announcement!

Please join me in recognizing and celebrating our Walnut Heights Teacher of the Year and Staff Member of the Year!

Alan Siegel - 4th Grade Teacher

Many of you with older students know Mr. Siegel. A talented, experienced educator, he strives to get to know his students each and every year, and instill in them all the necessary learning, but especially a love of reading.

Kurenn Hisamoto - Attendance Clerk & Campus Supervisor

If your child has ever been sick, absent or tardy, you have likely interacted with Ms. Kurenn. She is detail-oriented, attentive, patient and above all, kind.

Incoming 6th Grade - Welcome to WCI!

5th grade parents should have received an email from WCI Principal Jason Law on Sunday evening.

Welcome New Squire Families!

We are excited to welcome our incoming Class of 2026–2027 students & families to Walnut Creek Intermediate. Middle school is a big step, and while it can come with a few nerves, our goal is simple: Help your child feel confident, supported, and genuinely excited to become a Squire. It is time to be BRAVE!

As you start looking ahead to spring, we want to share a few important opportunities to get connected with WCI. A good place to start is OUR WEBSITE. In April, our administrative and counseling team will be visiting Walnut Heights students! These visits give us a chance to preview the WCI experience and get students excited about middle school. We are scheduled to visit Walnut Heights on April 14 at 1:45 pm.

Parents are invited to a parent-only informational night April 9 from 6:30–7:30 pm in the WCI gym. Learn about life at Walnut Creek Intermediate, ask questions, and get a sense of what the next three years will look like. Our PTA will be there to share how to stay involved and connected.

Because your child is already enrolled in a WCSD elementary school, there is nothing you need to do to enroll or register. Students will automatically transition to WCI. Sixth grade schedules are mostly predetermined, so there is not a traditional class selection process, with just a couple of exceptions that we will explain on April 9.

The unofficial (and my favorite) kickoff to the WCI experience happens on May 21 during our annual Open House. From 6:30 - 8:00 pm, families are invited to walk the campus, visit classrooms, and see firsthand what learning looks like at WCI. This is a great opportunity for both you and your child to experience the school together.

After spending years at your neighborhood elementary school, we know the transition to middle school can feel like a big leap, for students and parents alike. Please know that you are not doing this alone.

Welcome to Walnut Creek Intermediate.
Welcome to the Squire family.

Jason Law

CalendarEvent.description: A single calendar event extracted from an email.

start_datetime.description: The event start date and time (ISO 8601)
start_datetime.title: Start Datetime

end_datetime.description: The event end date and time (ISO 8601)
end_datetime.title: End Datetime

all_day.description: Whether this is an all-day event
all_day.title: All Day

description.description: Detailed description of the event with all relevant information
description.title: Description

confidence.description: Confidence score from 0.0 to 1.0 indicating extraction certainty
confidence.title: Confidence

importance.description: Event importance: 'action_required' for closures, schedule changes, deadlines the user must act on; 'fyi' for themed days, birthdays, informational items that are nice to know
importance.title: Importance

recurrence_rule.description: RFC 5545 RRULE for recurring events (e.g., 'RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO'). Null for one-time events.
recurrence_rule.title: Recurrence Rule

CalendarEvent.title: CalendarEvent

events_found.description: Whether any calendar events were found in the email
events_found.title: Events Found

events.title: Events
